2606.31495.title: Surprise as a Signal for Plasticity and Metacognition
2606.31495.paper_summary: We study a single idea across two settings: that a prediction-error signal, computed by a small predictor over the latent space of a frozen encoder, can serve both as a gate on plasticity and as a substrate for metacognition. In the first system, a non-parametric episodic memory writes a new concept only when this surprise is high, and a periodic offline replay phase consolidates recent traces into a slow linear readout. On a continual stream of 1000 ImageNet classes with a frozen DINOv2 or I-JEPA backbone, the consolidation phase recovers 17.7 points of retention on the oldest classes for DINOv2 and 51.3 points for I-JEPA (single-seed runs), and an ablation shows that replaying only a recent window is worse than no replay at all. In few-shot evaluation the same memory reaches 91.6% on 5-way 1-shot mini-ImageNet, above a task-specific baseline, while a harder 500-way regime exposes the true difficulty. In the second system, the same surprise signal, computed in a shared text-image space, modulates the behaviour of a vision-language model: it answers assertively when a concept is known, hedges when it is partially familiar, and refuses to identify the object and asks for an explanation when it is novel, learning the concept from a single user utterance. The external detector separates known from novel concepts at an AUROC of 0.966 (95% CI +/-0.024), far above the model's own verbalised confidence (0.618), while its token-level confidence sits below chance under greedy decoding; after a sleep phase that empties the fast store, the system recalls 99.2% of fifty taught facts from the consolidated store while a base model recovers none. We report both systems as proof-of-concept, with explicit limitations, and position the second against recent episodic-memory and personalised-VLM work.
2606.31495.paper_summary_zh: 我們在兩個設定中研究一個單一的想法：由一個小型預測器在凍結編碼器的潛在空間中計算的預測誤差信號，可以同時作為可塑性的閘和元認知的基質。在第一個系統中，非參數的情節記憶僅在驚訝度高時寫入新概念，而一個定期的離線重播階段將最近的痕跡整合成一個緩慢的線性讀出。在一個持續的1000個ImageNet類別的流中，使用凍結的DINOv2或I-JEPA骨幹，整合階段在DINOv2的最舊類別上恢復了17.7分的保留率，而在I-JEPA上則為51.3分（單種子運行），而一個消融實驗顯示僅重播最近的窗口比完全不重播更糟。在少量樣本評估中，同樣的記憶在5-way 1-shot mini-ImageNet上達到91.6%，高於特定任務的基準，而更困難的500-way範疇暴露了真正的難度。在第二個系統中，同樣的驚訝信號，在共享的文本-圖像空間中計算，調節了視覺-語言模型的行為：當一個概念已知時，它會自信地回答；當它部分熟悉時，則會有所保留；而當它是新穎的時候，則拒絕識別該物體並要求解釋，從單一用戶的話語中學習該概念。外部檢測器在0.966的AUROC下將已知與新穎概念分開（95% CI +/-0.024），遠高於模型自身的口頭信心（0.618），而其標記級別的信心在貪婪解碼下低於隨機；在一個清空快速存儲的睡眠階段之後，系統從整合存儲中回憶起99.2%的五十個教授事實，而基礎模型則未能恢復任何。我們報告這兩個系統作為概念驗證，並明確指出其限制，並將第二個系統與近期的情節記憶和個性化VLM工作進行對比。
2606.31495.author: Louis Mouchon et.al.
